string.text: Just Give Up Man Loaded Successfully.!
Thank you for coming, my {?PLAYER.GENDER}madame{?}sir{\?}.
I see your situation is dire. Do you want to surrender and spare your people from further suffering?
We are starving to death. The city is yours.
We can't hold on any longer. Please have mercy, the castle is yours.
The soldiers are on the verge of mutiny, there is no food left. We surrender.
We are grateful for your mercy!
As your new lord who saves you from starvation, I grant you mercy.
You made a wise choice. Lay down your arms, I spare your lives.
Kalradia will remember this cruelty!
Let's see if you can fight on an empty stomach.
It is too late to beg for mercy. I am coming to crush you.
STOP, We cannot fight you. We surrender!
We accept your surrender. Lay down your arms!
No, you all will be dead. Prepare!
A wise choice. You shall be spared.
Enemy forces have surrendered!
The battle continues!
{SETTLEMENT_NAME} wants to negotiate surrender with you.
The commander of {SETTLEMENT_NAME} wants to negotiate surrender with you.
The castellan of {SETTLEMENT_NAME} wants to negotiate surrender with you.
Surrender Negotiation
Accept Meeting
Reject Offer
Starvation has broken the siege stalemate; battle begins!
The starving defenders have launched a desperate sally out!
Offer a Negotiation Meeting
Your previous request is still awaiting a response.
You recently sent a negotiation request. Wait before sending another.
You need to wait before trying negotiation again after a failed persuasion.
Your messenger has been sent. You will receive a response soon.
Negotiation Response
Your proposal was accepted. A representative is ready to meet you.
Your proposal was rejected. The defenders do not believe your position is strong enough.
Go to meeting
We are listening. Speak your terms.[ib:nervous][if:convo_grave]
There is no need for more bloodshed. Hear me out.
The fate of these walls rests on your words.
That is not convincing.
Your words are not enough. We refuse.
Your people do not need to die for a hopeless defense.
Look at the balance of power. This siege is already decided.
Choose surrender, and your people can live.
Your people should not die in a hopeless defense.
Look at the numbers. This siege is already decided.
Choose the honorable path and spare your people.
Your walls are breaking. Prolonging this only wastes lives.
If this turns into an assault, you lose all leverage.
Open the gates and I will keep order over your civilians.
Surrender by treaty protects your honor before all Calradia.
Your supplies and morale decline while my advantage grows.
Do not trade lives for pride. End this with mercy.
A city survives by preserving people, not ruins.
Yield now and the markets reopen instead of burning.
Choose surrender, and history will remember restraint over slaughter.
This argument is no longer available.
Your Mercy is too low to make this argument.
Your Honor is too low to make this argument.
You need a more calculating approach to make this argument.
At least one wall section must be at half health or lower to use this argument.
You are right, there's no need for blood to spill.
No. We will not yield.
I need to hear more.
Just Give Up Man
General Surrender Tendency
Controls how willing enemies are to surrender overall. Higher values make surrenders happen more easily; lower values make them resist longer. Default: 1.0
Base Surrender Threshold
The minimum power advantage you need before enemies consider surrendering. Higher values mean you need to be much stronger. Default: 3.5
Surrender Randomness Mode
Determines how RNG is applied to surrender calculations. Off: purely based on thresholds. Thresholded: RNG applies between Base and Guaranteed thresholds. Unbound: slight RNG always applies.
Guaranteed Surrender Threshold
Power advantage required for guaranteed surrender without RNG (used in Thresholded and Unbound modes). Default: 5.0
Player Mercy Multiplier
How much your character's Mercy trait influences surrender chances. A merciful reputation makes enemies more willing to surrender; a cruel one makes them fight harder. Default: 100
Required Surrender Count
How many times an AI lord must surrender before they gain a Mercy trait point. Default: 3
Enable Siege Surrender
When enabled, besieged settlements can offer to surrender if conditions are dire enough. Disable to prevent any siege surrenders.
Enable Starvation Sally Out
When enabled, starving defenders who refuse to surrender may charge out of the walls in a desperate attack instead of waiting to die.
Nearby Enemy Lord Strength (%)
When hostile lords are near a besieged settlement, this percentage of their strength boosts the defenders' confidence, making them less likely to surrender. Default: 50
Nearby Lord Detection Range
How far the settlement looks for nearby lords on the map. A larger range means more lords are considered in surrender calculations. Default: 7
Siege Tower Pressure Multiplier
How much siege towers pressure defenders toward surrendering. Higher values make siege towers more intimidating. Default: 70
Ram Pressure Multiplier
How much battering rams pressure defenders toward surrendering. Higher values make rams more intimidating. Default: 70
Catapult Pressure Multiplier
How much catapults pressure defenders toward surrendering. Higher values push defenders closer to giving up. Default: 65
Trebuchet Pressure Multiplier
How much trebuchets pressure defenders toward surrendering. Higher values push defenders closer to giving up. Default: 65
Siege Engine Power Ratio Multiplier
How much having more siege engines than the defender affects surrender pressure. Higher values amplify the advantage of engine superiority. Default: 35
Wall Damage Pressure Multiplier
How much damaged walls increase surrender pressure. Crumbling walls make defenders feel exposed and more likely to give up. Default: 75
Destroyed Wall Bonus
Extra surrender pressure for each fully breached wall. More breaches mean greater pressure to surrender. Default: 0.2
Wall Damage Curve Exponent
Controls how wall damage scales. Higher values mean small cracks have little effect, but severe breaches cause a big jump in surrender pressure. Default: 1.5
Negotiation Easy Threshold
The power ratio at which persuasion becomes easy. When your army is this much stronger, convincing defenders is straightforward. Default: 3.0
Negotiation Normal Threshold
The power ratio for normal difficulty persuasion. You have a clear advantage but still need good arguments. Default: 2.2
Negotiation Hard Threshold
The power ratio for hard persuasion. Below this, defenders see little reason to negotiate. Default: 1.6
Persuasion Difficulty
Controls how hard it is to persuade defenders to surrender.
Enable Lord Surrender
When enabled, enemy lords you encounter on the map may surrender if you are much stronger. Disable to always force a battle.
Lord Dialog Priority
Technical: dialog priority for lord surrender lines. Higher values ensure JGUM takes precedence over other mods. Only change if you have mod conflicts. Default: 10000
Calculating Trait Multiplier
How much the defending lord's Calculating trait affects surrender. Calculating lords are more pragmatic and surrender more readily when outmatched. Default: 100
Valor Trait Multiplier
How much the defending lord's Valor trait affects surrender. Brave lords prefer to fight and are less willing to give up. Default: 100
Mercy Trait Multiplier
How much the defending lord's Mercy trait affects surrender. Merciful lords prefer to spare lives and surrender more willingly. Default: 100
Honor Trait Multiplier
How much the defending lord's Honor trait affects surrender. Honorable lords see surrender as shameful and resist longer. Default: 100
Enable Patrol Surrender
When enabled, non-lord enemy parties (patrols, caravans, etc.) may surrender when heavily outmatched. Disable to always force a battle.
Patrol Dialog Priority
Technical: dialog priority for patrol surrender lines. Higher values ensure JGUM takes precedence over other mods. Only change if you have mod conflicts. Default: 10000
